article.title: Topics priority for interviews.
article.content:
I am currently working as a Senior software developer for a product based company in Bangalore, planning to give interviews for top tier companies like salesforce, linkedin, atlassian, rippling, airbnb, amazon, microsoft, adobe, roku, confluent.

I have listed down some of the DSA topics i need to prepare, can u please share topics based on priority, with highest priority on top.

**Topics:**

backtracking
graphs
advanced graphs
1D dynamic programming
2D dynamic programming
greedy

Other topics like arrays, trees, linkedlist, stack, queue, heap etc i am already preparing so please ignore those.

post.content:
I currently work at meta and have interviewed and cracked almost all the companies you listed above .
The topics usually differ slightly if you compare but i wll try to list it down.

The best way is to check past company questions/topics when targeting a company - I will give a generic list first then give company wise fav topics:


General list-

1) Dynamic Programming (1d , 2d , with bit manipulation)
2) Graph. bfs /dfs  (imp but sometimes ignored - topological sort , union-find)
3) Binary Search
4) Backtracking /Recursion
5) Trie
6) Segment trees
7) Sliding window
8) Bit Manipulation

I know you mentioned stack and queue - but monotonic stack and monotonic queue is also something whic is asked alot.

Company wise observations -

1) Meta - does not ask dynamic programing , fav topics binary search , recursion
2)Google - Dynamic programing and topics like trie , segment trees , rare but sometimes red black tree
3) Atlassian - generic prep is ok , but their question pool is quite small , just try to get past asked questions from leetcode discussion and focus on it.
4) salesforce - (Ps i worked there before) - asks linked list and graph a lot .

Based on my interview experience, I'd recommend:

1. 1D dynamic programming
2. backtracking
3. graphs

Bit Manipulation is actually important for airbnb interviews too

- backtracking
- 1D dynamic programming
- 2D dynamic programming
Others are not that important at SSE level
